title: Silene subodhii
intro: Silene subodhii, also known as Himalayan Silene or Silene of the Himalayas, is a flowering plant species belonging to the carnation family (Caryophyllaceae). The plant has been traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine for its purported health benefits, including the treatment of fever, inflammation, and respiratory issues. Bioactive constituents isolated from Silene subodhii include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Herbal preparations made from Silene subodhii include decoctions, infusions, and extracts, which are often used to prepare medicinal teas and capsules for the relief of various health conditions.
taxonomy: Silene subodhii is classified within the family Caryophyllaceae. It belongs to the order Caryophyllales. The genus Silene falls under the family Caryophyllaceae and contains around 850 species. Silene subodhii is native to the Himalayas and is a flowering plant. More research is needed to confirm the full taxonomic classification of Silene subodhii.
